meta.title: Tabs
meta.description: Organizes content into distinct sections, enabling users to switch views within a single container.

mdx:
## Overview

Tabs are a navigational component used to organize and switch between different
sections of content within the same contextual view, allowing users to access
multiple related pages or views without navigating away from the main screen.

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/AvtPX6g7OGGCRvNlatGOIY/NIMBUS-design-system?node-id=8571-34966&m=dev)
[React Aria Docs](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/Tabs.html)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Direction for tabs

Tabs can be oriented horizontally or vertically to suit different layout
requirements.

#### Horizontal tabs

Organize and display distinct, related sections of content immediately beneath a
common navigation bar or header, serving primarily to group content contextually
while conserving vertical screen space.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="600">
    <Box>
      <Text fontWeight="bold" mb="300">Large</Text>
      <Tabs.Root size="lg">
        <Tabs.List>
          <Tabs.Tab id="general">General info</Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="history">History</Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="subscription">Subscription</Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="downloads">Downloads</Tabs.Tab>
        </Tabs.List>
        <Tabs.Panels pt="400">
          <Tabs.Panel id="general">General information content goes here.</Tabs.Panel>
          <Tabs.Panel id="history">History content goes here.</Tabs.Panel>
          <Tabs.Panel id="subscription">Subscription content goes here.</Tabs.Panel>
          <Tabs.Panel id="downloads">Downloads content goes here.</Tabs.Panel>
        </Tabs.Panels>
      </Tabs.Root>
    </Box>

    <Box>
      <Text fontWeight="bold" mb="300">Medium</Text>
      <Tabs.Root size="md">
        <Tabs.List>
          <Tabs.Tab id="general">General info</Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="history">History</Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="subscription">Subscription</Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="downloads">Downloads</Tabs.Tab>
        </Tabs.List>
        <Tabs.Panels pt="400">
          <Tabs.Panel id="general">General information content goes here.</Tabs.Panel>
          <Tabs.Panel id="history">History content goes here.</Tabs.Panel>
          <Tabs.Panel id="subscription">Subscription content goes here.</Tabs.Panel>
          <Tabs.Panel id="downloads">Downloads content goes here.</Tabs.Panel>
        </Tabs.Panels>
      </Tabs.Root>
    </Box>

    <Box>
      <Text fontWeight="bold" mb="300">Small</Text>
      <Tabs.Root size="sm">
        <Tabs.List>
          <Tabs.Tab id="general">General info</Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="history">History</Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="subscription">Subscription</Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="downloads">Downloads</Tabs.Tab>
        </Tabs.List>
        <Tabs.Panels pt="400">
          <Tabs.Panel id="general">General information content goes here.</Tabs.Panel>
          <Tabs.Panel id="history">History content goes here.</Tabs.Panel>
          <Tabs.Panel id="subscription">Subscription content goes here.</Tabs.Panel>
          <Tabs.Panel id="downloads">Downloads content goes here.</Tabs.Panel>
        </Tabs.Panels>
      </Tabs.Root>
    </Box>
  </Stack>
)
```

#### Vertical tabs

Maximize horizontal reading space and accommodate a larger number of
consistently visible options than traditional horizontal tabs allow.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="row" gap="1000">
    <Box flex="1">
      <Text fontWeight="bold" mb="300">Vertical left</Text>
      <Tabs.Root orientation="vertical" placement="start">
        <Tabs.List>
          <Tabs.Tab id="foryou">
            <Icons.SentimentSatisfied />
            For you
          </Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="recent">
            <Icons.Schedule />
            Recent
          </Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="starred">
            <Icons.Star />
            Starred
          </Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="apps">
            <Icons.Apps />
            Apps
          </Tabs.Tab>
        </Tabs.List>
        <Tabs.Panels>
          <Tabs.Panel id="foryou">For you content</Tabs.Panel>
          <Tabs.Panel id="recent">Recent content</Tabs.Panel>
          <Tabs.Panel id="starred">Starred content</Tabs.Panel>
          <Tabs.Panel id="apps">Apps content</Tabs.Panel>
        </Tabs.Panels>
      </Tabs.Root>
    </Box>

    <Box flex="1">
      <Text fontWeight="bold" mb="300">Vertical right</Text>
      <Tabs.Root orientation="vertical" placement="end">
        <Tabs.List>
          <Tabs.Tab id="foryou">
            <Icons.SentimentSatisfied />
            For you
          </Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="recent">
            <Icons.Schedule />
            Recent
          </Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="starred">
            <Icons.Star />
            Starred
          </Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="apps">
            <Icons.Apps />
            Apps
          </Tabs.Tab>
        </Tabs.List>
        <Tabs.Panels>
          <Tabs.Panel id="foryou">For you content</Tabs.Panel>
          <Tabs.Panel id="recent">Recent content</Tabs.Panel>
          <Tabs.Panel id="starred">Starred content</Tabs.Panel>
          <Tabs.Panel id="apps">Apps content</Tabs.Panel>
        </Tabs.Panels>
      </Tabs.Root>
    </Box>
  </Stack>
)
```

overview.mdx:
## Overview

Tabs are a navigational component used to organize and switch between different
sections of content within the same contextual view, allowing users to access
multiple related pages or views without navigating away from the main screen.

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/AvtPX6g7OGGCRvNlatGOIY/NIMBUS-design-system?node-id=8571-34966&m=dev)
[React Aria Docs](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/Tabs.html)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Direction for tabs

Tabs can be oriented horizontally or vertically to suit different layout
requirements.

#### Horizontal tabs

Organize and display distinct, related sections of content immediately beneath a
common navigation bar or header, serving primarily to group content contextually
while conserving vertical screen space.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
```

a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Tabs.Root defaultSelectedKey="history">
    <Tabs.List>
      <Tabs.Tab id="general">General info</Tabs.Tab>
      <Tabs.Tab id="history">History</Tabs.Tab>
      <Tabs.Tab id="subscription">Subscription</Tabs.Tab>
      <Tabs.Tab id="downloads">Downloads</Tabs.Tab>
    </Tabs.List>
    <Tabs.Panels pt="400">
      <Tabs.Panel id="general">
        <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
          <Text>Name: Jon Doe</Text>
          <Text>Email: Jon.Doe@domain.com</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Tabs.Panel>
      <Tabs.Panel id="history">
        <List.Root>
          <List.Item>03.15.25 - Jon Doe created an account.</List.Item>
          <List.Item>04.03.25 - Jon Doe updated profile information.</List.Item>
          <List.Item>06.06.25 - Jon Doe made their first purchase.</List.Item>
        </List.Root>
      </Tabs.Panel>
      <Tabs.Panel id="subscription">
        <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
          <Text>Current subscription: Coffee delivery subscription $15/mo</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Tabs.Panel>
      <Tabs.Panel id="downloads">
        <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
          <Text>No downloads available</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Tabs.Panel>
    </Tabs.Panels>
  </Tabs.Root>
)
```

### Accessibility standards

- **Keyboard navigation:** The Tab key should move focus to the entire tab list.
  The Left/Right Arrow keys must be used to navigate between tabs once the tab
  list is focused. Enter or Space selects the focused tab.
- **ARIA roles and states:**
  - The container must use `role="tablist"`.
  - Individual tabs must use `role="tab"` and `aria-selected="true/false"`.
  - The content area must use `role="tabpanel"` and be linked to its respective
    tab via `aria-labelledby`.
- **Focus management:** Ensure that when a tab is activated, focus moves
  logically to the associated tab panel or the first interactive element within
  it.
- **Contrast:** The contrast ratio between the active tab text and its
  background must be high, and the focus indicator must be easily visible.

dev.mdx: ## Getting started ### Import ```tsx import { Tabs, type TabsProps } from '@commercetools/nimbus'; ``` ### Basic usage The Tabs component supports two approaches depending on your needs. For maximum control over tab structure and content, use the **Compound API** with manual composition: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { return ( First Tab Second Tab Third Tab Content for the first tab. Content for the second tab. Content for the third tab. ); }; ``` For data-driven tabs that render from an array, use the **Dynamic rendering** approach: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { const tabs = [ { id: 'tab1', tabLabel: 'First Tab', panelContent: Content for the first tab., }, { id: 'tab2', tabLabel: 'Second Tab', panelContent: Content for the second tab., }, { id: 'tab3', tabLabel: 'Third Tab', panelContent: Content for the third tab., }, ]; return ; }; ``` When using the `tabs` prop, provide a `tabListAriaLabel` for accessibility. This label is applied to the tab list element for screen readers. ## Usage examples ### Size variants The Tabs component supports three size variants: `sm`, `md` (default), and `lg`. ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { const tabs = [ { id: '1', tabLabel: 'Overview', panelContent: 'Overview content' }, { id: '2', tabLabel: 'Details', panelContent: 'Details content' }, { id: '3', tabLabel: 'Settings', panelContent: 'Settings content' }, ]; return ( Small Medium (default) Large ); }; ``` ### Visual variants The Tabs component offers two visual styles: - **line** (default) - Tabs with an underline indicator - **pills** - Tabs with a pill-shaped background when active ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { const tabs = [ { id: '1', tabLabel: 'Dashboard', panelContent: 'Dashboard view' }, { id: '2', tabLabel: 'Analytics', panelContent: 'Analytics view' }, { id: '3', tabLabel: 'Reports', panelContent: 'Reports view' }, ]; return ( Line variant (default) Pills variant ); }; ``` ### Orientation Tabs can be displayed horizontally or vertically using the `orientation` prop. ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { return ( Horizontal (default) Home About Contact Welcome to our homepage! Learn more about us. Get in touch with us. Vertical Profile Security Notifications Manage your profile settings. Configure security options. Adjust notification preferences. ); }; ``` ### Placement The `placement` prop controls where the tab list appears relative to the panels: - **horizontal orientation**: `start` (left-aligned) or `end` (right-aligned) - **vertical orientation**: `start` (tabs on left) or `end` (tabs on right) ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { const tabs = [ { id: '1', tabLabel: 'First', panelContent: 'First panel content' }, { id: '2', tabLabel: 'Second', panelContent: 'Second panel content' }, { id: '3', tabLabel: 'Third', panelContent: 'Third panel content' }, ]; return ( Horizontal with placement="start" Horizontal with placement="end" Vertical with placement="start" (tabs on left) Vertical with placement="end" (tabs on right) ); }; ``` ### Disabled tabs Individual tabs can be disabled using the `disabledKeys` prop on `Tabs.Root`, or the `isDisabled` prop on individual `Tabs.Tab` components. ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { return ( Disabled via disabledKeys prop Active Tab Disabled Tab Another Active Tab First tab content is accessible. This content cannot be accessed. Third tab content is accessible. Disabled via isDisabled prop Available Unavailable Available This tab is enabled. This tab is disabled. This tab is also enabled. ); }; ``` ### Dynamic tabs with tabs prop For dynamic content, pass an array of tab configurations to the `tabs` prop. This is useful when tab data comes from an API or changes at runtime. ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { const [tabs, setTabs] = useState([ { id: '1', tabLabel: 'Introduction', panelContent: 'Welcome to our product documentation.', }, { id: '2', tabLabel: 'Getting Started', panelContent: 'Learn how to get started with our product.', }, { id: '3', tabLabel: 'API Reference', panelContent: 'Explore our comprehensive API documentation.', }, ]); const addTab = () => { const newId = String(tabs.length + 1); setTabs([ ...tabs, { id: newId, tabLabel: `Tab ${newId}`, panelContent: `Dynamic content for tab ${newId}`, }, ]); }; const removeLastTab = () => { if (tabs.length > 1) { setTabs(tabs.slice(0, -1)); } }; return ( ); }; ``` ### Manual composition with compound API Use the compound API for more control over tab and panel rendering, including custom content and complex layouts. ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { return ( Overview Analytics Settings Dashboard Overview View your key metrics and recent activity at a glance. Analytics & Reports Dive deep into your data with comprehensive analytics tools. Configuration Settings Customize your experience with advanced settings options. ); }; ``` ### Uncontrolled mode By default, Tabs operates in uncontrolled mode where it manages its own state. Use `defaultSelectedKey` to set the initially selected tab. ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { return ( Tab 1 Tab 2 (Initially Selected) Tab 3 First tab content Second tab content - selected by default Third tab content ); }; ``` ### Controlled mode For full control over tab selection, use the `selectedKey` and `onSelectionChange` props. ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { const [selectedTab, setSelectedTab] = useState('profile'); return ( Currently selected tab: {String(selectedTab)} Profile Account Preferences Manage your profile information View and edit account settings Customize your preferences ); }; ``` ## Component requirements ## Accessibility The Tabs component follows WCAG 2.1 AA guidelines and implements the WAI-ARIA tabs pattern. #### Role - `Tabs.Root` contains the tablist and tabpanels - `Tabs.List` has `role="tablist"` - `Tabs.Tab` has `role="tab"` with `aria-selected` indicating active state - `Tabs.Panel` has `role="tabpanel"` and is associated with its tab via `aria-labelledby` #### Labeling - Each tab must have an `id` prop that matches its corresponding panel - Tab content should be descriptive and concise - Complex tab labels can include icons and text - When using the `tabs` prop API, provide `tabListAriaLabel` to label the tab list for screen readers - When using the compound API, apply `aria-label` directly to `Tabs.List` #### Keyboard navigation | Key | Action | |-----|--------| | `Tab` | Move focus into and out of the tab list | | `ArrowRight` / `ArrowDown` | Move focus to the next tab (horizontal/vertical) | | `ArrowLeft` / `ArrowUp` | Move focus to the previous tab (horizontal/vertical) | | `Home` | Move focus to the first tab | | `End` | Move focus to the last tab | | `Enter` / `Space` | Activate the focused tab (when `keyboardActivation="manual"`) | #### Persistent ID If your use case requires tracking and analytics for this component, it is good practice to add a **persistent**, **unique** id to the component: ```tsx const PERSISTENT_ID = "user-settings-tabs"; export const SettingsTabs = () => ( Profile Security Profile content Security content ); ``` ## API reference ## Common patterns ### Tab content with icons Combine icons with text labels for more expressive tabs. ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { return ( Dashboard Reports Settings Dashboard content with key metrics and visualizations. Generate and view comprehensive reports. Configure application settings and preferences. ); }; ``` ### Tabs with badges Display notification counts or status indicators alongside tab labels using badges. ```jsx live-dev const App = () => { return ( Inbox 12 Drafts 3 Sent Spam 2 You have 12 new messages in your inbox. You have 3 draft messages. View your sent messages. 2 messages marked as spam. ); }; ``` ## Testing your implementation These examples demonstrate how to test your implementation when using Tabs within your application. As the component's internal functionality is already tested by Nimbus, these patterns help you verify your integration and application-specific logic. ### Basic Rendering Tests Verify the Tabs component renders with expected structure and accessibility attributes ```tsx import React from "react"; import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"; import { Tabs, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("Tabs - Basic rendering", () => { it("renders tabs with correct structure", () => { render( First Tab Second Tab Third Tab First panel content Second panel content Third panel content ); // Verify tablist is present expect(screen.getByRole("tablist")).toBeInTheDocument(); // Verify all tabs are rendered expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "First Tab" })).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Second Tab" })).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Third Tab" })).toBeInTheDocument(); // Verify first tab is selected by default expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "First Tab" })).toHaveAttribute( "aria-selected", "true" ); // Verify corresponding panel is visible expect(screen.getByRole("tabpanel")).toHaveTextContent( "First panel content" ); }); it("renders tabs with dynamic tabs prop", () => { const tabs = [ { id: "1", tabLabel: "Overview", panelContent: "Overview content" }, { id: "2", tabLabel: "Details", panelContent: "Details content" }, { id: "3", tabLabel: "Settings", panelContent: "Settings content" }, ]; render( ); expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Overview" })).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Details" })).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Settings" })).toBeInTheDocument(); }); it("applies size variants correctly", () => { const { rerender } = render( Tab 1 Content ); const tabsRoot = screen.getByTestId("tabs"); expect(tabsRoot).toBeInTheDocument(); // Rerender with different size rerender( Tab 1 Content ); expect(screen.getByTestId("tabs")).toBeInTheDocument(); }); it("applies visual variants correctly", () => { const { rerender } = render( Tab 1 Content ); expect(screen.getByTestId("tabs")).toBeInTheDocument(); // Rerender with pills variant rerender( Tab 1 Content ); expect(screen.getByTestId("tabs")).toBeInTheDocument(); }); }); ``` ### Interaction Tests Test user interactions including clicking tabs and keyboard navigation ```tsx import React from "react"; import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"; import { Tabs, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("Tabs - Interactions", () => { it("switches tabs when clicked", async () => { const user = userEvent.setup(); render( First Second Third First content Second content Third content ); // Initially first tab is selected expect(screen.getByRole("tabpanel")).toHaveTextContent("First content"); // Click second tab await user.click(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Second" })); // Verify second tab is now selected expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Second" })).toHaveAttribute( "aria-selected", "true" ); expect(screen.getByRole("tabpanel")).toHaveTextContent("Second content"); // Click third tab await user.click(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Third" })); // Verify third tab is now selected expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Third" })).toHaveAttribute( "aria-selected", "true" ); expect(screen.getByRole("tabpanel")).toHaveTextContent("Third content"); }); it("supports keyboard navigation with arrow keys", async () => { const user = userEvent.setup(); render( First Second Third First content Second content Third content ); const firstTab = screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "First" }); const secondTab = screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Second" }); const thirdTab = screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Third" }); // Use userEvent.tab() to focus first tab (avoids act() warnings) await user.tab(); expect(firstTab).toHaveFocus(); // Navigate to second tab with ArrowRight await user.keyboard("{ArrowRight}"); expect(secondTab).toHaveFocus(); // Navigate to third tab with ArrowRight await user.keyboard("{ArrowRight}"); expect(thirdTab).toHaveFocus(); // Navigate back to second tab with ArrowLeft await user.keyboard("{ArrowLeft}"); expect(secondTab).toHaveFocus(); // Activate with Enter await user.keyboard("{Enter}"); expect(secondTab).toHaveAttribute("aria-selected", "true"); }); it("supports Home and End keys for navigation", async () => { const user = userEvent.setup(); render( First Second Third First content Second content Third content ); const firstTab = screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "First" }); const thirdTab = screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Third" }); // Use userEvent.tab() to focus first tab (avoids act() warnings) await user.tab(); // Press End to jump to last tab await user.keyboard("{End}"); expect(thirdTab).toHaveFocus(); // Press Home to jump to first tab await user.keyboard("{Home}"); expect(firstTab).toHaveFocus(); }); }); ``` ### Controlled Mode Tests Test controlled mode with selectedKey and onSelectionChange props ```tsx import React from "react"; import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"; import { Tabs, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("Tabs - Controlled mode", () => { it("handles controlled mode with selectedKey and onSelectionChange", async () => { const user = userEvent.setup(); const handleSelectionChange = vi.fn(); const ControlledTabs = () => { const [selectedKey, setSelectedKey] = React.useState( "tab1" ); return ( { setSelectedKey(key); handleSelectionChange(key); }} > First Second Third First content Second content Third content ); }; render( ); // Initially first tab is selected expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "First" })).toHaveAttribute( "aria-selected", "true" ); // Click second tab await user.click(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Second" })); // Verify callback was called expect(handleSelectionChange).toHaveBeenCalledWith("tab2"); // Verify second tab is now selected expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Second" })).toHaveAttribute( "aria-selected", "true" ); }); it("respects defaultSelectedKey for uncontrolled mode", () => { render( First Second Third First content Second content Third content ); // Second tab should be selected by default expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Second" })).toHaveAttribute( "aria-selected", "true" ); expect(screen.getByRole("tabpanel")).toHaveTextContent("Second content"); }); }); ``` ### Disabled States Tests Test disabled tabs via disabledKeys prop and isDisabled prop ```tsx import React from "react"; import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"; import { Tabs, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("Tabs - Disabled states", () => { it("disables tabs via disabledKeys prop", async () => { const user = userEvent.setup(); render( First Second (Disabled) Third First content Second content Third content ); const disabledTab = screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Second (Disabled)", }); // Verify tab is disabled expect(disabledTab).toHaveAttribute("aria-disabled", "true"); // Try to click disabled tab await user.click(disabledTab); // Verify disabled tab did not become selected expect(disabledTab).not.toHaveAttribute("aria-selected", "true"); // First tab should still be selected expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "First" })).toHaveAttribute( "aria-selected", "true" ); }); it("disables individual tabs via isDisabled prop", async () => { const user = userEvent.setup(); render( Available Unavailable Available First content Second content Third content ); const disabledTab = screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Unavailable" }); // Verify tab is disabled expect(disabledTab).toHaveAttribute("aria-disabled", "true"); // Try to click disabled tab await user.click(disabledTab); // Verify disabled tab did not become selected expect(disabledTab).not.toHaveAttribute("aria-selected", "true"); }); }); ``` ### Dynamic Tabs Tests Test dynamic tab rendering and updates ```tsx import React from "react"; import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"; import { Tabs, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("Tabs - Dynamic tabs", () => { it("updates when tabs array changes", () => { const initialTabs = [ { id: "1", tabLabel: "First", panelContent: "First content" }, { id: "2", tabLabel: "Second", panelContent: "Second content" }, ]; const { rerender } = render( ); // Verify initial tabs expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "First" })).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Second" })).toBeInTheDocument(); // Update tabs array const updatedTabs = [ ...initialTabs, { id: "3", tabLabel: "Third", panelContent: "Third content" }, ]; rerender( ); // Verify new tab is rendered expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "First" })).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Second" })).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(screen.getByRole("tab", { name: "Third" })).toBeInTheDocument(); }); it("handles empty tabs array gracefully", () => { const { container } = render( ); // Component should render without errors expect(container).toBeInTheDocument(); }); }); ``` ## Resources - [Tabs Storybook](https://nimbus-storybook.vercel.app/?path=/docs/components-tabs--docs) - [React Aria Tabs Documentation](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/Tabs.html) - [WAI-ARIA Tabs Pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/tabs/)

guidelines.mdx:
## Guidelines

Provides a structured, predictable pattern for displaying multiple panes of
related content in the same area. It minimizes screen clutter by allowing
content sections to be easily hidden and revealed, typically used for navigation
within a product or configuration panel.

### Best practices

- **Consistency and context:** Ensure all content within the tab panels is
  closely related to the parent context (e.g., all tabs relate to "User
  Settings" or "Product Details").
- **Concise labels:** Keep tab labels short (one or two words is ideal) and
  descriptive, clearly indicating the content of the corresponding panel.
- **Maintain visibility:** Ensure the current active tab is clearly
  distinguishable from inactive tabs using strong visual cues (e.g., accent
  color, bold text, contrasting underline).
- **Maintain content position:** Do not use tabs to reorganize or filter the
  content of a single continuous list. The content associated with each tab
  should be unique and distinct.
- **Limit number of tabs:** Avoid creating a large number of tabs (ideally 3–7
  visible tabs) to prevent horizontal scrolling or overcrowding, which harms
  usability. Use a dropdown menu or alternative navigation pattern for overflow.
- **Avoid sub-navigation:** Do not place secondary navigation elements (like
  another row of tabs) directly inside the tab panel unless absolutely
  necessary.

### Usage

Tabs are used to organize related content into distinct, switchable sections,
allowing users to efficiently access different views within the same area
without leaving the context of the main page.

> [!TIP]\
> When to use

- **Organizing related views:** To switch between different views of the same
  object (e.g., "Details," "Inventory," "History" tabs on a Product page).
- **Configuration settings:** For grouping diverse settings categories (e.g.,
  "General," "Billing," "Security" sections within a settings page).
- **Conserving vertical space:** To maximize vertical screen space by presenting
  options horizontally.

> [!CAUTION]\
> When not to use

- **Primary site navigation:** Do not use tabs for navigating between major,
  top-level sections of an application (use main navigation menus or links
  instead).
- **Too many options:** If the number of tabs causes horizontal scrolling or
  requires hiding options in an overflow menu on desktop.
- **Linear workflows:** Avoid using tabs to guide users through a mandatory,
  step-by-step process.

### Tab labeling

> [!TIP]\
> **Do**
>
> - Choose concise, descriptive labels (1-2 words) that accurately preview the
>   content within the panel to aid quick visual scanning.
> - Ensure all tab content is closely related to the parent screen's context,
>   maintaining a single-page feel.
> - Implement an overflow strategy (like a "More" menu) if the tab count exceeds
>   7, rather than allowing horizontal scrolling, which is poor usability.
> - Verify that content inside the hidden tab panels remains available to screen
>   readers, aligning with accessibility best practices.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Tabs.Root defaultSelectedKey="history">
    <Tabs.List>
      <Tabs.Tab id="general">General info</Tabs.Tab>
      <Tabs.Tab id="history">History</Tabs.Tab>
      <Tabs.Tab id="subscription">Subscription</Tabs.Tab>
      <Tabs.Tab id="downloads">Downloads</Tabs.Tab>
    </Tabs.List>
    <Tabs.Panels pt="400">
      <Tabs.Panel id="general">
        <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
          <Text>Name: Jon Doe</Text>
          <Text>Email: Jon.Doe@domain.com</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Tabs.Panel>
      <Tabs.Panel id="history">
        <List.Root>
          <List.Item>03.15.25 - Jon Doe created an account.</List.Item>
          <List.Item>04.03.25 - Jon Doe updated profile information.</List.Item>
          <List.Item>06.06.25 - Jon Doe made their first purchase.</List.Item>
        </List.Root>
      </Tabs.Panel>
      <Tabs.Panel id="subscription">
        <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
          <Text>Current subscription: Coffee delivery subscription $15/mo</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Tabs.Panel>
      <Tabs.Panel id="downloads">
        <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
          <Text>No downloads available</Text>
        </Stack>
      </Tabs.Panel>
    </Tabs.Panels>
  </Tabs.Root>
)
```

> [!CAUTION]\
> **Don't**
>
> - Don't use tabs for site-wide or primary application navigation; reserve them
>   for internal to pages, contextual navigation.
> - Don't place tabs on the screen if they cause the content area below them to
>   feel disconnected or unrelated.
> - Don't use tabs to reorganize the content of a single continuous list; the
>   content under each tab should be unique and distinct.
> - Don't use overly long, descriptive sentences as tab labels, as they clutter
>   the interface and harm quick visual scanning.
